title: eulobus angelorum
intro: Eulobus angelorum, also known as California poppy or golden poppy, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and act as a mild sedative. The health benefits of Eulobus angelorum include its potential to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and infusing the flowers or leaves in hot water to make a tea, which can also be used as a tincture or added to salves and ointments for topical use.
